body:
### Description

oxlint's cmd function
```lua
  cmd = function(dispatchers, config)
    local cmd = 'oxlint'
    if (config or {}).root_dir then
      local local_cmd = vim.fs.joinpath(config.root_dir, 'node_modules/.bin', cmd)
      if vim.fn.executable(local_cmd) == 1 then
        cmd = local_cmd
      end
    end
    return vim.lsp.rpc.start({ cmd, '--lsp' }, dispatchers)
  end,
```
does not check for if nvim is running under win32, and tries to spawn using the bash version instead.
Here's the error code:
```
...s64/ucrt64/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_transport.lua:68: Spawning language server with cmd: `{ "C:/path/to/some/projects/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }` failed. The language server is either not installed, missing from PATH, or not executable.
```
I'm using the default configurations from nvim-lspconfig, only capabilities and on_init functions have been modified.

This fix has worked for me so far
```lua
  cmd = function(dispatchers, config)
    local cmd = 'oxlint'
    if (config or {}).root_dir then
      local ext = (vim.fn.has('win32') == 1) and '.cmd' or ''
      local local_cmd = vim.fs.joinpath(config.root_dir, 'node_modules/.bin', cmd .. ext)
      if vim.fn.executable(local_cmd) == 1 then
        cmd = local_cmd
      end
    end
    return vim.lsp.rpc.start({ cmd, '--lsp' }, dispatchers)
  end,
```

nvim version
NVIM v0.12.2
Build type: Release
LuaJIT 2.1.1774896198
Run "nvim -V1 -v" for more info

Windows build:
10.0.19045 Build 19045

I'm using MSYS2 in UCRT64 runtime. I installed neovim from the command `pacman -S mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-neovim`
I don't know the amount of support neovim has for msys2...
bash `uname`: `MINGW64_NT-10.0-19045`

`set shell?` = "C:/msys64/usr/bin/bash.exe"

Minimal project configuration to repro:
```bash
mkdir issue-39298
cd issue-39298
pnpm init
pnpm add -D oxlint typescript
cat <<EOF > oxlint.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "oxlint";

export default defineConfig({

})
EOF
mkdir src
cd src
cat <<EOF > index.ts
function main() {
    console.log("Hello, World!");
}
EOF
nvim index.ts
```

Running 
```lua
:lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" })
```

```
E5108: Lua: ...s64/ucrt64/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_transport.lua:68: Spawning language server with cmd: `{ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }` failed.
 The language server is either not installed, missing from PATH, or not executable.
stack traceback:
        [C]: in function 'error'
        ...s64/ucrt64/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_transport.lua:68: in function 'run'
        C:/msys64/ucrt64/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/rpc.lua:668: in function 'start'
        [string ":lua"]:1: in main chunk
```

comment_data.body: But it checks `vim.fn.executable() == 1`. So this is more like a problem in `rpc.start()` ?

I have edited the issue,

```
Running MSYS2 bash before opening neovim:
    Manually:
      - vim.o.shell = "C:\\msys64\\usr\\bin\\bash.exe" -- Default
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "-c"
        vim.o.shellxquote = ""

        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }) -> ❌ Error
        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint.CMD", "--lsp" }) -> ✅ No error

      - vim.o.shell = "cmd.exe"
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "/s /c"
        vim.o.shellxquote = "\""

        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }) -> ✅ No error
        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint.CMD", "--lsp" }) -> ✅ No error

    Automatically (opening a .ts file):
      - vim.o.shell = "C:\\msys64\\usr\\bin\\bash.exe" -- Default
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "-c"
        vim.o.shellxquote = ""

        cmd: C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint
        result: ❌ ...s64/ucrt64/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_transport.lua:68: Spawning language server with cmd: `{ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }` failed. The language server is either not installed, missing from PATH, or not executable.

      - vim.o.shell = "cmd.exe"
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "/s /c"
        vim.o.shellxquote = "\""

        cmd: C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint
        result: ✅ No error

Running Powershell before opening neovim:
    Manually:
      - vim.o.shell = "cmd.exe" -- Default
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "/s /c" -- Default
        vim.o.shellxquote = "\"" -- Default

        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }) -> ✅ No error
        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint.CMD", "--lsp" }) -> ✅ No error

      - vim.o.shell = "C:\\msys64\\usr\\bin\\bash.exe"
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "-c"
        vim.o.shellxquote = ""

        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }) -> ❌ Error
        :lua vim.lsp.rpc.start({ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint.CMD", "--lsp" }) -> ✅ No error

     Automatically (opening a .ts file):
      - vim.o.shell = "cmd.exe" -- Default
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "/s /c" -- Default
        vim.o.shellxquote = "\"" -- Default

        cmd: C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint
        result: ✅ No error

      - vim.o.shell = "C:\\msys64\\usr\\bin\\bash.exe"
        vim.o.shellcmdflag = "-c"
        vim.o.shellxquote = ""

        cmd: C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint
        result: ❌ ...s64/ucrt64/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/_transport.lua:68: Spawning language server with cmd: `{ "C:/msys64/home/User/issue-39298/node_modules/.bin/oxlint", "--lsp" }` failed. The language server is either not installed, missing from PATH, or not executable.

```

Something is wrong with msys's bash with vim.lsp.rpc...

```
In `node_modules/.bin/` directory:

with files: ["oxlint", "oxlint.CMD"]
cmd.exe:
    >>> .\oxlint
    ✅ No error
    >>> .\oxlint.CMD
    ✅ No error
bash.exe:
    >>> ./oxlint
    ✅ No error
    >>> ./oxlint.CMD
    ✅ No error

with files: ["oxlint"]
cmd.exe:
    >>> .\oxlint
    '.\oxlint' is not recognized as an internal or external command,
    operable program or batch file.
    >>> .\oxlint.CMD
    '.\oxlint.CMD' is not recognized as an internal or external command,
    operable program or batch file.
bash.exe:
    >>> ./oxlint
    ✅ No error
    >>> ./oxlint.CMD
    bash: ./oxlint.CMD: No such file or directory

with files: ["oxlint.CMD"]
cmd.exe:
    >>> .\oxlint
    ✅ No error
    >>> .\oxlint.CMD
    ✅ No error
bash.exe:
    >>> ./oxlint
    bash: ./oxlint: No such file or directory
    >>> ./oxlint.CMD
    ✅ No error
```

Seems like cmd.exe appends .CMD whenever possible.

This also highlights the fact that under MSYS2, with a default of using bash.exe as the shell doesn't also set shellcmdflag to "-c"...

I confirmed this by printing the vim.o.shell, vim.o.shellcmdflag and vim.o.shellxquote in init.lua.

title: Windows: vim.lsp.rpc.start() does not execute `.cmd`
